response: {"calibration_score":0.31,"overconfident_count":4,"underconfident_count":2,"well_calibrated_count":8,"biases_detected":["anchoring — repeated same failing command pattern (cp without -f, tsc via node_modules/.bin) before checking preconditions","re-edit thrash — reading index.ts 3-6 times per unit without retaining state, matching known [re-edit-thrash] pattern","familiarity bias — assumed node_modules/.bin/tsc present before checking; fell back to bun only after two failures","optimism bias — gum table piped input attempted twice with same broken syntax before switching approach"],"recommendations":["Before any file-copy or shell command that has a known failure mode, check preconditions first (directory exists, binary present) rather than retrying with minor flag variations","Reduce redundant file reads per unit: read index.ts once, extract all needed ranges, then plan edits — do not interleave read/edit/read/edit on the same file without a clear hypothesis change","When a bash command fails twice with similar syntax, treat second failure as a signal to investigate root cause (binary path, syntax, pipe format) rather than issuing a third near-identical attempt","Cache tsc/bun/gum capability checks at session start rather than rediscovering them mid-unit"]}
